codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of reusable `renderAreaChart()` in components.js with gradient fills, dots, labels, and judgment-state coloring via `getJudgmentColors()`. Homepage sparklines replaced with 120x48px area charts in the movement board and 80x40px charts in the watchlist. Direction detail page gains a prominent 14-day trend chart (800x180px) with axis labels, data point dots, and gradient fill. All charts are pure SVG with viewBox. No external libraries. Code is well-documented with JSDoc. Verified on both jade and streetwear data per the handoff.

issues.description: getJudgmentColors(dir.judgment_state) is called twice in the watchlist card template — once for fillColor and once for strokeColor. Should be extracted to a local variable for minor efficiency and readability, similar to how renderMovementChart() does it.

The detail page chart width uses `Math.min(800, window.innerWidth - 200)` which is evaluated once at render time and won't update on window resize. Since the SVG uses viewBox this isn't a visual bug, but the width attribute will be stale if the user resizes their browser. Acceptable for a static dashboard.
Gradient ID uses `Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 9)` which is fine for uniqueness but `substr` is deprecated in favor of `substring`. Non-blocking since it works in all current browsers.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The new `getJudgmentColors()` helper duplicates the judgment state color mapping that also exists as CSS custom properties in style.css and as inline hex values in several other chart functions (e.g., renderJudgmentDonutChart in index.html). AGENTS.md documents the CSS variable names and hex codes, but doesn't note that `getJudgmentColors()` in components.js is now the canonical JS helper for these mappings. Future workers may create their own color mapping instead of reusing it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: dashboard/js/components.js:412 defines getJudgmentColors(); index.html:340 also defines inline color arrays for the donut chart. AGENTS.md lists hex codes under 'SVG Charts' section but doesn't reference the JS helper.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `renderAreaChart()` function is now a shared utility in components.js but AGENTS.md doesn't document it as an available reusable component. Future visual features may recreate chart rendering instead of reusing this function.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: dashboard/js/components.js:294 — renderAreaChart() with full JSDoc. Used by index.html (movement board + watchlist) and direction-detail.html (14-day trend). Not mentioned in AGENTS.md or library docs.

summary: Implementation fully meets all expected behaviors. Homepage movement board uses 120x48px area charts (≥120×48 required), direction detail page has 800x180px trend chart (≥300×150 required) with gradient fill, data point dots, and axis labels. Charts use judgment-state coloring, are pure SVG with no external libraries, and work with both jade and streetwear data. Three non-blocking style issues noted (duplicate getJudgmentColors call, one-time window.innerWidth evaluation, deprecated substr). Two shared-state observations about documenting the new reusable chart functions.
